info.title: Model Gateway
info.description:
# Overview

The Model Gateway is the unified inference gateway for the Agentic AI Platform. It provides OpenAI-compatible APIs for chat completions, embeddings, and image generation across multiple cloud providers with automatic failover, content safety validation, and enterprise-grade resilience.

## Environment URLs

| Environment | Model Gateway (APIM) | TID Authorization Server |
|-------------|---------------------|--------------------------|
| **Development** | `https://models.dev.trimble-ai.com` | `https://stage.id.trimblecloud.com` |
| **Staging** | `https://models.stage.trimble-ai.com` | `https://stage.id.trimblecloud.com` |
| **Production** | `https://models.ai.trimble.com` | `https://id.trimble.com` |

**Base Path:** All OpenAI-compatible endpoints use the `/openai/v1` base path.

**Full Example:** `{{BASE_URL}}/openai/v1/chat/completions`

> **Note:** Throughout this documentation, `{{BASE_URL}}` refers to the Model Gateway URL for your target environment (e.g., `https://models.dev.trimble-ai.com` for development).

## Getting Started

The API is protected by OAuth2.0 and requires a valid TID access token in the Authorization header. Refer to [TID Authentication Guide](https://developer.trimble.com/docs/authentication) for details.

**Required TID Token Scopes:**
- `openid` - Get user identity
- `models` - Use AI model inference endpoints
- `profile` - Get user profile information

## Key Architecture Concepts

### Unified Model IDs

The Model Gateway uses **unified model identifiers** that abstract away cloud provider details. When you request `model: "gpt-4o"`, the system automatically:

1. Looks up the model in the deployment catalog
2. Retrieves the multi-cloud deployment configuration
3. Transforms the unified ID to provider-specific deployment names (e.g., `gpt-4o` → `gpt-4o-dzs` for Azure)
4. Routes to the appropriate cloud provider with failover support

This means you use a single model name regardless of which cloud provider serves the request.

### Multi-Cloud Failover

Requests are routed through a **priority-based failover chain**:

```
Request (model="claude-4.5-sonnet")
  ↓
Try Azure (priority=1)
  ├─ Try eastus2 → Try westus → Try all 11 regions...
  └─ If ALL regions fail → Try next provider
  ↓
Try Vertex AI (priority=2)
  ├─ Try global location → Try other locations...
  └─ Return first success
```

**Failover Behavior:**
- **Client errors (4xx)**: No failover - the error indicates an invalid request
- **408 Request Timeout**: Triggers failover - may succeed on another provider
- **429 Rate Limit**: Triggers failover - another provider may have capacity
- **5xx Server Errors**: Triggers failover - provider is having issues

---

## Content Safety (Model Guard Integration)

All inference requests are validated through **Model Guard** before being sent to AI providers. This provides:

- **Harmful Content Detection**: Analyzes text for hate speech, violence, sexual content, self-harm, and harassment
- **Jailbreak Detection**: Identifies attempts to bypass AI safety guidelines
- **Prompt Injection Detection**: Catches hidden instructions and manipulation attempts
- **Image Safety Analysis**: Validates images for explicit, violent, or harmful visual content

**When Content is Blocked:**
- Returns HTTP 400 (Bad Request) with `type: "content_policy_violation"` and `code: "content_blocked"`
- The `recommendation` field indicates the action: `allow`, `review`, or `block`
- The `flagged_categories` array lists which safety categories triggered the block
- Violation records are stored in Model Guard for audit when risk score >= 0.3

**Configuration:**
- `MODEL_GUARD_ENABLED`: Enable/disable content safety (default: true)
- `MODEL_GUARD_URL`: Model Guard service URL
- `MODEL_GUARD_TIMEOUT`: Timeout in seconds (default: 10, max: 60)

---

## Resilience & High Availability

### Regional Load Balancing

Each cloud provider implements regional load balancing with health-aware routing:

- **Strategy**: Round-robin with health awareness (configurable)
- **Health Tracking**: Per-endpoint success/failure tracking
  - Unhealthy threshold: 3 consecutive failures mark endpoint unhealthy
  - Healthy threshold: 2 consecutive successes mark endpoint healthy
- **Auto-Recovery**: Endpoints automatically recover when healthy

### Circuit Breaker Pattern

Each regional endpoint has its own circuit breaker:

- **Failure Threshold**: 3 failures opens the circuit
- **Recovery Timeout**: 30 seconds before half-open state
- **Half-Open**: Allows test requests to check if endpoint recovered

### Retry with Exponential Backoff

Retryable errors trigger automatic retries:

- **First Retry**: Immediate (first-fast-retry)
- **Subsequent Retries**: Exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 3s... up to 10s max)
- **Retryable Errors**: 5xx errors, 429 rate limit, connection errors

---

## Security & Validation Differences from OpenAI

### Image Editing: Pre-Upload Required

**Unlike standard OpenAI**, the Model Gateway does **not** accept base64-encoded images directly in image editing requests. This is a security measure to ensure:

1. All images pass through content safety analysis
2. Images are stored with audit trail and metadata
3. Domain allowlist prevents arbitrary external image access

**Image Editing Workflow:**

```bash
# Step 1: Upload your image
curl -X POST {{BASE_URL}}/openai/v1/files \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -F "file=@source_image.png" \
  -F "purpose=image-edit"

# Response includes the signed content URL:
# { "url": "{{BASE_URL}}/v1/files/file_abc123/content?exp=...&sig=..." }

# Step 2: Use the returned URL in your edit request
curl -X POST {{BASE_URL}}/openai/v1/images/edits \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model": "gpt-image-1",
    "image": "{{BASE_URL}}/v1/files/file_abc123/content?exp=...&sig=...",
    "prompt": "Add a rainbow in the sky"
  }'
```

**Validation Rules:**
- Only `http://` or `https://` URLs accepted (no `data:` URIs, no base64)
- URL must end with `/content` path (from `/v1/files` upload)
- Domain must be in the allowlist (configured per environment)

---

## Provider-Specific Features (extra_body)

The `extra_body` parameter allows passing provider-specific options that aren't part of the standard OpenAI API.

### Anthropic Claude Models

Enable extended thinking for complex reasoning tasks:

```json
{
  "model": "claude-3-5-sonnet",
  "messages": [...],
  "extra_body": {
    "thinking": {
      "type": "enabled",
      "budget_tokens": 2048
    },
    "metadata": {
      "user_id": "user-123"
    },
    "service_tier": "priority",
    "top_k": 40
  }
}
```

**Supported Parameters:**
| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `thinking` | Extended thinking configuration `{"type": "enabled", "budget_tokens": N}` |
| `metadata` | Request metadata for tracking |
| `service_tier` | Priority capacity selection (`priority`, `standard`) |
| `mcp_servers` | MCP servers array for tool use |
| `container` | Container identifier for session reuse |
| `top_k` | Top-K sampling parameter |

**Note**: Thinking blocks are filtered from responses - they don't appear in OpenAI format output.

### Gemini Models (Chat & Image Generation)

Configure safety settings and Gemini-specific parameters:

```json
{
  "model": "gemini-2.0-flash",
  "messages": [...],
  "extra_body": {
    "safetySettings": [
      {
        "category": "HARM_CATEGORY_DANGEROUS_CONTENT",
        "threshold": "BLOCK_ONLY_HIGH"
      }
    ],
    "thinking_level": "medium",
    "topK": 40,
    "topP": 0.95
  }
}
```

**Supported Parameters:**
| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `safetySettings` | Array of safety filter configurations |
| `thinking_level` | For Gemini 3: `"low"`, `"medium"`, `"high"` |
| `topK` | Top-K sampling (alternative to top_p) |
| `topP` | Nucleus sampling parameter |
| `imageSize` | For image generation: `"1K"`, `"2K"`, `"4K"` |

### Gemini Image Generation (via Chat Completions)

Gemini can generate images through the chat completions endpoint with `responseModalities`:

```json
{
  "model": "gemini-2.0-flash",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Generate an image of a futuristic cityscape at sunset"
    }
  ],
  "extra_body": {
    "generationConfig": {
      "responseModalities": ["TEXT", "IMAGE"],
      "imageConfig": {
        "aspectRatio": "16:9",
        "imageSize": "2K"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

**Recursive Image Editing with Gemini:**

Use chat completions for iterative image editing - send the previous output back as input:

```json
{
  "model": "gemini-2.5-flash-image",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": [
        {"type": "text", "text": "Make the sky more purple and add stars"},
        {
          "type": "image_url",
          "image_url": {
            "url": "{{BASE_URL}}/v1/files/file_previous/content?..."
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "extra_body": {
    "generationConfig": {
      "responseModalities": ["TEXT", "IMAGE"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Imagen Models

Google Imagen supports extensive customization:

```json
{
  "model": "imagen-3",
  "prompt": "A serene mountain landscape",
  "extra_body": {
    "negativePrompt": "blurry, low quality, distorted",
    "seed": 12345,
    "addWatermark": false,
    "sampleImageSize": "2K",
    "enhancePrompt": true,
    "personGeneration": "allow_adult",
    "safetySetting": "block_some",
    "language": "en",
    "includeRaiReason": true
  }
}
```

**Supported Parameters:**
| Parameter | Description | Imagen 3 | Imagen 4 |
|-----------|-------------|----------|----------|
| `negativePrompt` | What to avoid in generation | Yes | No |
| `seed` | Deterministic generation seed | Yes | Yes |
| `addWatermark` | Add SynthID watermark (default: true) | Yes | Yes |
| `sampleImageSize` | Output size: `"1K"`, `"2K"` | No | Yes |
| `enhancePrompt` | Auto-enhance prompt | No | Yes |
| `personGeneration` | `"dont_allow"`, `"allow_adult"`, `"allow_all"` | Yes | Yes |
| `safetySetting` | `"block_most"`, `"block_some"`, `"block_few"` | Yes | Yes |
| `language` | Prompt language code | Yes | Yes |
| `includeRaiReason` | Include safety filter reasons | Yes | Yes |
| `storageUri` | GCS URI for direct output storage | Yes | Yes |

### Fal.ai Models (FLUX, Ideogram)

> **Restricted Access:** Fal.ai models are currently restricted to the SketchUp division only. Contact the platform team if you need access.

```json
{
  "model": "fal-ai/flux-pro/v1.1-ultra",
  "prompt": "A photorealistic portrait",
  "extra_body": {
    "guidance_scale": 7.5,
    "num_inference_steps": 50,
    "scheduler": "DPMSolverMultistep",
    "sync_mode": true,
    "loras": [
      {"url": "https://...", "scale": 0.8}
    ]
  }
}
```

**Supported Parameters:**
| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `guidance_scale` | Classifier-free guidance scale |
| `num_inference_steps` | Number of denoising steps |
| `scheduler` | Diffusion scheduler algorithm |
| `sync_mode` | Wait for completion vs return job ID |
| `loras` | Array of LoRA models to apply |

---

## Access Restrictions

Model Gateway access is restricted to the following services:

- **Agent Service** - AI agent orchestration and tool use
- **KB Service** - Knowledge base and document retrieval
- **N8n** - Workflow automation

---

## Error Handling

All errors follow OpenAI error format:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "message": "Detailed error description",
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "code": "invalid_image_format",
    "param": "image"
  }
}
```

**Common Error Types:**
| HTTP Status | Error Type | Description |
|-------------|------------|-------------|
| 400 | `invalid_request_error` | Malformed request or invalid parameters |
| 400 | `content_policy_violation` | Content blocked by Model Guard (code: `content_blocked`) |
| 401 | `authentication_error` | Missing or invalid token |
| 403 | `permission_denied_error` | Insufficient permissions |
| 404 | `not_found_error` | Model or resource not found |
| 429 | `rate_limit_error` | Rate limit exceeded |
| 500 | `server_error` | Internal server error |
| 502 | `bad_gateway` | Upstream provider error |
| 503 | `service_unavailable` | All providers failed |
| 504 | `timeout_error` | Model Guard timeout (code: `guard_timeout`) |

---

## Observability

All requests include comprehensive OpenTelemetry instrumentation:

**Trace Attributes:**
- `model.unified_id`: Requested model name
- `model.vendor`: Model vendor (OpenAI, Google, Anthropic)
- `model.capabilities`: Model capabilities (chat, embeddings, images)
- `failover.chain_length`: Number of providers in failover chain
- `failover.successful_provider`: Provider that served the request
- `failover.attempts`: Number of providers tried

**Metadata Headers:**
Pass these headers for correlation and tracking:
- `x-request-id`: Client-provided request ID
- `x-session-id`: Session identifier
- `x-agents-agentId`, `x-agents-threadId`, `x-agents-runId`: Agent orchestration tracking
- `x-kb-libraryId`, `x-kb-documentId`: Knowledge base tracking

---

## API Endpoints Reference

### OpenAI-Compatible Endpoints

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| `/openai/v1/chat/completions` | POST | Create chat completion (supports streaming) |
| `/openai/v1/embeddings` | POST | Generate text embeddings |
| `/openai/v1/images/generations` | POST | Generate images from text prompt |
| `/openai/v1/images/edits` | POST | Edit images with text prompt |
| `/openai/v1/models` | GET | List all available models |

### File Management Endpoints

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| `/v1/files` | POST | Create file upload slot (returns `uploadUrl` + `url`) |
| `/v1/files/{id}` | GET | Get file metadata (owner only, JWT auth, `?includeContentUrl=true` for fresh `contentUrl`) |
| `/v1/files/{id}/content` | GET | Access file content (signature auth, redirects to Azure SAS) |

**Response Fields:**
- `contentUrl` - 1-hour validity URL for accessing file content (only included when `?includeContentUrl=true`)

**File Upload Flow:**
1. POST `/v1/files` with `{"filename": "image.png", "purpose": "image-edit"}` → Returns `uploadUrl` and `url`
2. PUT file content directly to `uploadUrl` (Azure Blob SAS URL)
3. Use the `url` (render URL with signature) in image editing requests

---

## Support

- **OpenAPI Specification**: `/openai/v1/openapi.json`
- **Interactive Documentation**: `/openai/v1/docs`
- **ReDoc Documentation**: `/openai/v1/redoc`
- **Technical Support**: agentic-ai-platform@trimble.com

post.description:
Create a chat completion using OpenAI-compatible API.

This endpoint provides an OpenAI-compatible interface for chat completions,
routing requests to appropriate LLM providers (OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, etc.).

The endpoint uses official OpenAI SDK types to ensure 100% compatibility with
the OpenAI API specification.

Authentication:
- TID JWT token (required)

Args:
    request: The FastAPI request object
    body: The chat completion request body (OpenAI SDK format)
    user: Authenticated user information (from TID JWT)
    chat_service: The chat completions service instance

Returns:
    JSONResponse with OpenAI-compatible chat completion response,
    or StreamingResponse if stream=True

Raises:
    HTTPException: If authentication fails or request is invalid

Create embeddings using OpenAI-compatible API.

This endpoint provides an OpenAI-compatible interface for embeddings,
routing requests to appropriate embedding providers (OpenAI, Google, etc.).

The endpoint accepts all parameters from the OpenAI SDK's EmbeddingCreateParams
type, ensuring 100% compatibility with the official OpenAI API.

Authentication:
- TID JWT token (required)

Args:
    request: The FastAPI request object
    body: The embedding request (matches OpenAI SDK types)
    user: Authenticated user information (from TID JWT)
    embeddings_service: The embeddings service instance

Returns:
    JSONResponse with OpenAI-compatible embedding response

Raises:
    HTTPException: If authentication fails or request is invalid

Generate images using OpenAI-compatible API.

This endpoint provides an OpenAI-compatible interface for image generation,
routing requests to Google Vertex AI models (Imagen, Gemini).

Authentication:
- TID JWT token (required)

Args:
    request: The FastAPI request object
    body: The image generation request body (OpenAI format)
    user: Authenticated user information (from TID JWT)
    image_service: The image generation service instance

Returns:
    JSONResponse with OpenAI-compatible image generation response

Raises:
    HTTPException: If authentication fails or request is invalid

Edit images using OpenAI-compatible API.

This endpoint provides an OpenAI-compatible interface for image editing,
routing requests to Azure OpenAI GPT image models or Fal.ai FLUX edit models.

For Gemini image editing, use the chat completions endpoint with
responseModalities: ["TEXT", "IMAGE"] instead.

Authentication:
- TID JWT token (required)

Args:
    request: The FastAPI request object
    body: The image edit request body (OpenAI format)
    user: Authenticated user information (from TID JWT)
    edit_service: The image edit service instance

Returns:
    JSONResponse with OpenAI-compatible image edit response

Raises:
    HTTPException: If authentication fails or request is invalid

get.description:
List all available models.

Returns a list of models available for use with the API,
in OpenAI-compatible format.

Authentication:
- TID JWT token (required)

Args:
    user: Authenticated user information (from TID JWT)
    models_service: The models service instance (injected)

Returns:
    OpenAIModelListResponse with list of available models

Raises:
    HTTPException: If authentication fails or service is unavailable

post.description:
Create a message using the native Anthropic Messages API.

This endpoint provides a zero-conversion passthrough to Anthropic-compatible
cloud providers, preserving all native features including prompt caching,
extended thinking, structured outputs, and tool use.

Authentication:
- TID JWT token (required)

Args:
    request: The FastAPI request object
    body: The Anthropic Messages request body (native format)
    user: Authenticated user information (from TID JWT)
    messages_service: The Anthropic messages service instance

Returns:
    JSONResponse with Anthropic Messages response,
    or StreamingResponse if stream=True

get.description: Returns paginated list of files owned by authenticated user. Supports FIQL filtering, pagination, and sorting.

parameters.name: includeFailed

schema.description: Include failed attempts (4xx errors) in results
schema.title: Includefailed

parameters.description: Include failed attempts (4xx errors) in results

parameters.name: includeProviderRequest

schema.description: Include provider request body in response
schema.title: Includeproviderrequest

parameters.description: Include provider request body in response

post.description: Creates a file record and returns URLs for upload and access. Upload your file directly to uploadUrl (PUT request with file content), then use the returned url in image editing requests.

get.description: Returns file metadata. Only the file owner can access this endpoint. Use ?includeContentUrl=true to get a 1-hour validity content URL.

parameters.name: includeContentUrl

schema.description: Include the canonical content URL in response (requires TID JWT to access)
schema.title: Includecontenturl

parameters.description: Include the canonical content URL in response (requires TID JWT to access)

parameters.name: includeProviderRequest

schema.description: Include provider request body in response
schema.title: Includeproviderrequest

parameters.description: Include provider request body in response

get.description:
Access file content via dual authentication. Supports two modes:
- **New (JWT)**: No query params. Requires `Authorization: Bearer <TID-JWT>`. JWT `sub` must match the file owner's `trimble_id`.
- **Legacy (HMAC)**: Provide `exp` and `sig` query params. No JWT needed. Backward-compatible with old signed URLs.

Redirects (302) to a fresh Azure SAS URL.

400.description: Incomplete legacy params (only one of exp/sig)

401.description: JWT required but missing or invalid

404.description: File not found or not yet uploaded

stream.title: Stream
stream.description: Whether to stream the response via SSE

system.title: System
system.description: System prompt (string or content blocks with cache_control)

tool_choice.title: Tool Choice
tool_choice.description: How the model should use tools

AnthropicMessagesRequest.title: AnthropicMessagesRequest
AnthropicMessagesRequest.description:
Native Anthropic Messages API request.

Required fields match the Anthropic Messages API specification:
- model: The model identifier
- messages: Conversation messages
- max_tokens: Maximum tokens to generate

All other Anthropic parameters (thinking, system, tools, metadata, etc.)
are accepted via extra="allow" for full passthrough without SDK lag.

See: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/messages

model.title: Model
model.description: ID of the model to use

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: A list of messages comprising the conversation so far. Uses OpenAI SDK's ChatCompletionMessageParam type.

parallel_tool_calls.title: Parallel Tool Calls
parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether to enable parallel function calling

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: List of tools the model may call

extra_body.title: Extra Body
extra_body.description: Additional provider-specific parameters (e.g., Gemini safety_settings, Anthropic thinking)

ChatCompletionRequest.title: ChatCompletionRequest
ChatCompletionRequest.description:
OpenAI-compatible chat completion request.

Mirrors openai.types.chat.completion_create_params.CompletionCreateParams
to ensure 100% API compatibility while adding FastAPI/Pydantic benefits.

model.title: Model
model.description: ID of the model to use

input.title: Input
input.description: Input text to embed, encoded as a string or array of strings/tokens

dimensions.title: Dimensions
dimensions.description: The number of dimensions the resulting output embeddings should have

encoding_format.title: Encoding Format
encoding_format.description: The format to return the embeddings in (float or base64)

user.title: User
user.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user

EmbeddingRequest.title: EmbeddingRequest
EmbeddingRequest.description:
OpenAI-compatible embeddings request.

Mirrors openai.types.embedding_create_params.EmbeddingCreateParams
to ensure 100% API compatibility while adding FastAPI/Pydantic benefits.

sizeBytes.title: Sizebytes
sizeBytes.description: File size in bytes (set after upload)

status.title: Status
status.description: File status for uploaded files: pending_upload, uploaded (None for generated files)

createdAt.title: Createdat
createdAt.description: When the file record was created

uploadedAt.title: Uploadedat
uploadedAt.description: When the file was uploaded (if known)

uploadExpiresAt.title: Uploadexpiresat
uploadExpiresAt.description: When the upload URL expires (for user-uploaded files only)

urlExpiresAt.title: Urlexpiresat
urlExpiresAt.description: When the content URL expires (only when content_url is included)

modelId.title: Modelid
modelId.description: Model that generated or processed the file

providerRequestBody.title: Providerrequestbody
providerRequestBody.description: Provider-specific request body sent to upstream AI service

errorStatusCode.title: Errorstatuscode
errorStatusCode.description: HTTP status code for failed attempts

FilePurpose.title: FilePurpose
FilePurpose.description:
Purpose of a file (API format with hyphens).

This enum defines the external API format for file purposes.
Internal operation_type uses underscores (e.g., "image_edit").

contentType.title: Contenttype
contentType.description: MIME type (auto-detected from filename if not provided)

FileUploadRequest.title: FileUploadRequest
FileUploadRequest.description: Request to create a file upload slot.

uploadUrl.title: Uploadurl
uploadUrl.description: Pre-signed URL via model-files domain for direct upload

url.title: Url
url.description: Canonical content URL for image editing. Requires TID JWT Authorization header to access.

urlExpiresAt.title: Urlexpiresat
urlExpiresAt.description: Content URL expiry (None for canonical JWT-auth URLs)

createdAt.title: Createdat
createdAt.description: When the file record was created

FileUploadResponse.title: FileUploadResponse
FileUploadResponse.description: Response with upload URL and canonical content URL.

image.title: Image
image.description: The image(s) to edit. Can be a URL, base64-encoded string, data URI, or an array of up to 10 images. For GPT models: png, webp, or jpg files less than 50MB each. For Fal.ai: publicly accessible URLs.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: A text description of the desired edit(s)

model.title: Model
model.description: The model to use for image editing

mask.title: Mask
mask.description: An additional image whose fully transparent areas indicate where the original image should be edited. Must be a valid PNG file with the same dimensions as the input image. Only supported by GPT models.

n.title: N
n.description: The number of images to generate (1-10, default varies by model)

size.title: Size
size.description:
The size of the generated images. Accepts two formats:
- Dimension format (WxH): 1024x1024, 1536x1024, 1024x1536, etc.
- Preset enum: auto, square_hd, square, portrait_4_3, etc.

OpenAI/GPT models: dimension format (e.g., 1024x1024) or 'auto'.
Fal.ai models: preset enum (square_hd, portrait_4_3, landscape_16_9).

quality.title: Quality
quality.description: Image quality. GPT models: low/medium/high/auto. Default varies by model.

response_format.title: Response Format
response_format.description: The format in which the generated images are returned. Must be 'url' or 'b64_json'. URLs are valid for 60 minutes.

user.title: User
user.description: A unique identifier for the end-user

background.title: Background
background.description: Background transparency: transparent, opaque, or auto. Only supported by GPT models. If transparent, output_format should be png or webp.

input_fidelity.title: Input Fidelity
input_fidelity.description: Control how much effort to match input image style/features. Supports 'high' or 'low'. Only supported by gpt-image-1.

output_format.title: Output Format
output_format.description: Output image format: png, jpeg, or webp (GPT models only)

output_compression.title: Output Compression
output_compression.description: Compression level 0-100 for jpeg/webp output. Only supported by GPT models. Default is 100.

num_inference_steps.title: Num Inference Steps
num_inference_steps.description: Number of inference steps (Fal.ai only, default: 4)

enable_safety_checker.title: Enable Safety Checker
enable_safety_checker.description: Enable safety checker (Fal.ai only, default: true)

extra_body.title: Extra Body
extra_body.description: Additional provider-specific parameters (e.g., Fal.ai guidance_scale, GPT moderation settings)

ImageEditRequest.title: ImageEditRequest
ImageEditRequest.description:
OpenAI-compatible image edit request.

Supports multiple image editing models:
- GPT image models (gpt-image-1, gpt-image-1-mini, gpt-image-1.5)
- Fal.ai models (FLUX.2 edit models)

The unified contract follows OpenAI's /v1/images/edits API specification,
with provider-specific parameters available via extra_body.

Note: For Gemini image editing, use the chat completions endpoint
with responseModalities: ["TEXT", "IMAGE"] instead.

model.title: Model
model.description: The model to use for image generation

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: A text description of the desired image(s)

n.title: N
n.description: The number of images to generate (1-10, default varies by model)

size.title: Size
size.description: The size of the generated images (default varies by model)

quality.title: Quality
quality.description: Image quality. GPT models: low/medium/high/auto. DALL-E 3: standard/hd. Imagen: standard/hd. FLUX: standard/hd. Default varies by model.

response_format.title: Response Format
response_format.description: Format of images: only 'url' is supported (presigned URL)

style.title: Style
style.description: Image style: natural or vivid (DALL-E 3 only)

user.title: User
user.description: A unique identifier for the end-user

output_format.title: Output Format
output_format.description: Output image format: png or jpeg (GPT models only)

output_compression.title: Output Compression
output_compression.description: Compression level 0-100 for JPEG output (GPT models only)

background.title: Background
background.description: Background type: transparent, opaque, or auto (GPT models only)

negative_prompt.title: Negative Prompt
negative_prompt.description: What to avoid in generated images (Imagen 3.0, FLUX.2-pro)

person_generation.title: Person Generation
person_generation.description: Person generation control: dont_allow, allow_adult, allow_all (Imagen only)

add_watermark.title: Add Watermark
add_watermark.description: Add SynthID watermark to images (Imagen only)

language.title: Language
language.description: Prompt language: auto, en, zh, zh-TW, hi, ja, ko, pt, es (Imagen only)

safety_setting.title: Safety Setting
safety_setting.description: Safety filter level: block_low_and_above, block_medium_and_above, block_only_high, block_none (Imagen only)

storage_uri.title: Storage Uri
storage_uri.description: Google Cloud Storage URI for output (Imagen only)

include_rai_reason.title: Include Rai Reason
include_rai_reason.description: Include RAI reason in response (Imagen only)

include_safety_attributes.title: Include Safety Attributes
include_safety_attributes.description: Include safety attributes in response (Imagen only)

guidance_scale.title: Guidance Scale
guidance_scale.description: How closely to follow the prompt (FLUX models). Higher values = stricter adherence. Range: 1.0-20.0, default: 3.5

num_inference_steps.title: Num Inference Steps
num_inference_steps.description: Number of denoising steps (FLUX models). More steps = higher quality but slower. Range: 1-100, default: 28

scheduler.title: Scheduler
scheduler.description: Denoising scheduler algorithm (FLUX models). Options: 'auto', 'euler', 'dpm++'. Default: 'auto'

width.title: Width
width.description: Custom image width in pixels (FLUX models). Must be multiple of 8. Range: 256-2048

height.title: Height
height.description: Custom image height in pixels (FLUX models). Must be multiple of 8. Range: 256-2048

strength.title: Strength
strength.description: Image editing strength (FLUX.1-Kontext-pro, FLUX.2-pro editing mode). 0 = no change, 1 = complete regeneration. Default: 0.8

moderation.title: Moderation
moderation.description: Content moderation level: low, auto (GPT models only)

extra_body.title: Extra Body
extra_body.description: Additional provider-specific parameters (e.g., Imagen safety_settings, Fal.ai guidance_scale)

ImageGenerationRequest.title: ImageGenerationRequest
ImageGenerationRequest.description:
OpenAI-compatible image generation request.

Supports multiple image generation models:
- GPT image models (gpt-image-1, gpt-image-1-mini, gpt-image-1.5)
- FLUX models (FLUX.1-Kontext-pro, FLUX-1.1-pro, FLUX.2-pro) via Azure AI Foundry
- Vertex AI models (Imagen, Gemini)
- Fal.ai models (flux-dev, Recraft, Stable Diffusion)

Note: Defaults are intentionally None to allow each model provider
to use its own defaults. This prevents sending unsupported parameter
values to APIs that have different requirements.

created.title: Created
created.description: Unix timestamp when the model was created

owned_by.title: Owned By
owned_by.description: The organization that owns the model

OpenAIModel.title: OpenAIModel
OpenAIModel.description:
OpenAI-compatible model object.

Mirrors the Model object from OpenAI's /v1/models endpoint.

OpenAIModelListResponse.title: OpenAIModelListResponse
OpenAIModelListResponse.description:
OpenAI-compatible model list response.

Mirrors the response from OpenAI's GET /v1/models endpoint.

href.title: Href
href.description: URL to the page. Contains encoded skipToken for cursor-based pagination.

PageLink.title: PageLink
PageLink.description: Link to a page in paginated responses.

data.title: Data
data.description: Array of items in the current page.

moreItemsAvailable.title: Moreitemsavailable
moreItemsAvailable.description: Indicates if more items are available beyond the current page. Used in certain APIs where links.next.href is always returned.

totalCount.title: Totalcount
totalCount.description: Total number of items across all pages (optional, may not always be provided).

next.description: Link to the next page of results. Absent if no more results.

prev.description: Link to the previous page of results. Absent on first page.

self.description: Link to the current page of results.
